description: Canonical acronym expansions referenced by the Category Artifact Meaning Layer (data/asset-meanings.json). Each entry documents a fixed or candidate expansion, the domains that use it, and any misreading a buyer should avoid.

terms.definition: The fundamental computing concept describing data flow into and out of a system, device, or process.
terms.caution: Not 'Isle of...', not an Indian Ocean reference, not a two-letter place abbreviation.

terms.definition: The standardized format for exchanging business documents — purchase orders, invoices, shipping notices — directly between organizations' computer systems.
terms.caution: Not DEI (Diversity, Equity, Inclusion) despite the visual similarity.

terms.definition: The classic marketing funnel model. AIDAtanaly reads as AIDA + T + Analy — AIDA Transition Analytics — measuring the transition between funnel stages, not just occupancy within them.
terms.caution: The correct source acronym is this marketing funnel, not a fusion of the words Artificial, Intelligence, Data, and Analysis.

terms.definition: A machine learning architecture loosely modeled on biological neural networks, foundational to modern deep learning.
terms.caution: Not a given name; not an abbreviation for 'announcement.'

terms.definition: Computer-generated simulated environments. ToAVR segments as To + A + VR, read aloud as 'to a VR' — naming the transition into a virtual reality environment.
terms.caution: The domain is not read as the three-letter acronym AVR (Audio/Video Receiver) — the correct segmentation splits the article 'A' from 'VR.'

terms.definition: The hardware and software systems that monitor and control physical industrial processes and devices, as distinct from conventional IT.
terms.caution: PayOTIS segments as Pay + OT + IS, not Pay + OTIS as a single unit — do not read OTIS as a standalone acronym.

terms.definition: A fixed technical expansion naming an advanced process concerned with the distal (downstream) end of a nascent (newly forming) chain during transcription — one word per letter.
terms.caution: Not 'Call To Action' (CTA) despite those letters appearing inside the acronym. Not an existing government regulator or ticker. The expansion is fixed, not left open for buyer interpretation.

terms.definition: Computational systems that perform tasks associated with human intelligence: pattern recognition, inference, and decision-making.
terms.caution: In AIElectronicChips, AI names the semiconductor category purpose-built for AI workloads — the claim is compute sovereignty, not a consumer chip storefront.

terms.definition: Standard finance and banking shorthand for currency, used in account, treasury, and FX systems to denote the monetary unit of denomination.
terms.caution: Not an invented syllable — Ccy is conventional finance-industry shorthand. Not limited to cryptocurrency; it applies to any currency, fiat or crypto.

terms.definition: A hypothesized class of AI that exceeds human intelligence across virtually all domains, one horizon beyond AGI.
terms.caution: Not an unrelated industry abbreviation or ticker; ASI here always names artificial super intelligence.

terms.definition: AI with generalized cognitive capability across domains, comparable to human general intelligence, one horizon short of ASI.
terms.caution: Not a generic AI-startup label — in AGIBeget, AGI is treated as a genesis/origin event, not a product feature.

terms.definition: The Ethereum blockchain platform and its native cryptocurrency.
terms.caution: Names the blockchain/cryptocurrency, not an unrelated institution or abbreviation sharing the same three letters.

terms.definition: Technology that overlays computer-generated content onto a user's real-world view in real time.
terms.caution: Not 'Art' — ARTopSight segments as AR + TOP + Sight, not an art/operations reading.

terms.definition: An AI architecture that retrieves relevant external information at query time and supplies it to a generative model to ground its output.
terms.caution: Not an unrelated acronym (e.g. red-amber-green status reporting) despite the shared letters, and not a textile 'rag' brand.

terms.definition: Orbital infrastructure that collects solar energy in space and transmits it to Earth or in-space consumers, unfiltered by atmosphere.
terms.caution: Not 'Static Site Pages', 'Server-Side Processing', or any unrelated tech acronym — SSP here is always the space-energy category.

terms.definition: Optimizing the full search journey — relevance, intent match, on-page experience, and AI-answer readiness — beyond legacy keyword/backlink SEO.
terms.caution: Not a stock ticker or unrelated abbreviation; distinct from, and broader than, traditional SEO.
